steps.title: Express length in terms of width using the ratio
steps.content: The ratio 9:4 tells us that $\frac{L}{W} = \frac{9}{4} = 2.25$. So length is always 2.25 times the width.

steps.title: Apply the width change to find the new length
steps.content:
When width increases from $W$ to $W + 6$, the new length must maintain the same relationship:
$$L_{\text{new}} = 2.25(W + 6) = 2.25W + 13.5$$
Since the original length is $L = 2.25W$, we have $L_{\text{new}} = L + 13.5$.

The length must increase by 13.5 units. C is correct.
